text: Excuse me, sir. Sir, how did you get rich? How did I get rich? You know what this building is? New York Stock Exchange? Yes. That's where I got rich. What do you do? I'm a trader on the floor of the Stock Exchange. Are you serious? Yeah. I've been doing it for 40 years. I'm the longest-standing broker in the world, the most famous stock broker in the world. How long have you been on Wall Street? You know who I am, don't you? Yes, that's right. Does he make a lot of money? How much money do you make? I trade between a half a billion and a billion dollars of stock every day. Every single day? Every day. I go all over the world to bring the richest people to help the younger generation. Can I get admitted with you for my children? 100%. You have more knowledge of money than 99.9% of people in the world. Give me a lesson about money that is not taught in school, that you install in everybody watching this right now. So, one of the things I always talk about, and I love talking to younger people, because they're the greatest consumer generation in the world. And one of the most important things is to invest in stocks and not stuff. Right? We love to buy stuff. Pretty much most things we buy goes down in value the minute you buy it. It's not rocket science. You know what? No. Think about it. When younger people ask me, what should I buy? And I'll say to them, go back to high school, walk down the corridor, and look at what sneakers everyone's wearing, what phones they use, what computers they're on, what they do in their spare time, and what social media they're on. Buy a little bit of each one of those companies. There's so much value in that. It's amazing. I know you interview. I've seen you before. I know you interview billionaires who have said, you know, how do you become rich? It's letting your money work for you. There's a number out there that says at the age of 18, if you put $250 a month into the S&P 500, right? Which is a basket of 500 stocks. At the age of 60, you'll have more than a million dollars. Passive investing. What's your name? Peter Tuckman. Peter, if mean you die tomorrow and you have one more God in principle to leave with the younger generation, what would that be? Now, I'll tell you something. I always get choked up. I've been doing this for 40 years. I've been through so much being broke for three years. But losing my brother, my parents, my wife died of cancer last year. Gone through so much. I almost died of COVID. I was given three months to live. What was that one thing that kept you going? Because you've been through so much adversity in the last. That I love what I do. I don't need to post a bunch of pictures of me on the back of a Bugatti with stacks of 10,000s. But if I can share what I found, I don't want to be on the mountaintop all by myself. Find something you love to do, get really good at it, and embrace the younger generation. You got amazing advice, sir. You're wonderful. Thank you so much for your time. Can I get your contact? Absolutely.
